explicit_text: Before my family and I started our journey to America, we had a big celebration in Play Baw Lu. Many relatives, including my grandmother, cousins, uncles, and aunts, gathered to help and say goodbye. I remember my grandmother didn’t say anything when she left that night, which made me wonder because she had always been close to me and cared for me since I was a baby. It was a bittersweet moment, filled with happiness and sadness as we prepared to leave.

explicit_text: When we arrived in Mae Sot, I met many new people who were also planning to go to America. We stayed in a camp there, but I didn’t like the smell, especially the bathroom, which was very pungent. Thankfully, we only stayed in Mae Sot for about a week before moving on. It was a difficult place but an important stop on our journey.
implicit_text: When we arrived at the border town in western Thailand, I met many new people who were also planning to go to America. explicit_text: Arriving in Bangkok, the capital of Thailand, was an eye-opening experience for me. I saw many cars and roads for the first time while looking out the window of a double-decker bus. We stayed in a hotel with many other families from the camp, and the next morning we woke up very early to continue our journey. Bangkok felt busy and exciting compared to where we had been before.

explicit_text: Thailand was a significant part of our journey to America. We traveled through places like Mae Sot and Bangkok, both located in Thailand, where we stayed in camps and hotels. The country was a transit point for many families like ours who were preparing to start a new life abroad. Thailand’s cities and camps marked important stages in our long trip.

explicit_text: Our flight took us to Hong Kong, where I saw many airplanes for the first time. I didn’t enjoy the plane ride and felt uneasy during the trip. Hong Kong was just a stopover on our way to America, but it left an impression on me because of the busy airport and the number of planes around.

explicit_text: When we arrived in Los Angeles, California, it felt somewhat familiar because it had many cars and roads like Bangkok, but the language and alphabet on the signs were different. My family and I stayed in a hotel for a night before moving on to another place. Los Angeles was our first stop in America, and it made me feel excited about this new chapter in our lives.

explicit_text: California was the state where we first landed in America, specifically in Los Angeles. Although we only stayed briefly, California marked the beginning of our new life in America.

explicit_text: After Los Angeles, my family and I moved to Twin Falls, Idaho, where I had the chance to attend school for the first time in America. There were not many Karen people there, and jobs were scarce, which made life challenging. We lived in Twin Falls for about six months before moving to another city.

explicit_text: St. Paul, Minnesota is where my family and I moved after living in Twin Falls. It is where I am now, and I feel more settled here. Moving to St. Paul was an important step in our journey as we continued to build our new life in America.
